scope_evaluation.reason: The full business idea encompasses order syncing, item retrieval, batching, SKU grouping, review identification, duplicate prevention, pick/pack document generation, PDF generation, DHL/Easy Ship label generation, a staff dashboard, and logging — spanning multiple UI surfaces, backend services, and third-party API integrations. This is an epic-level scope requiring multiple implementation cycles.
scope_evaluation.recommendation: Treat the full idea as an Epic. The first implementation candidate should be scoped to: 'FBM Order Grouping & Review Dashboard (Mock Data)' — a single-page dashboard that loads mock unshipped orders, groups them by SKU/quantity patterns, flags orders needing review, prevents duplicate processing, and displays grouped results. No PDFs, no real APIs, no label generation.

problem_statement: Every morning, operations staff manually review unshipped Amazon.de FBM orders, retrieve order items, separate orders by SKU and quantity patterns, prepare picking and packing documents, and generate shipping labels. This manual process is time-consuming, creates duplicate-work risk, and leads to fulfillment mistakes when orders contain mixed SKUs, repeated products, or require special review. There is no structured workflow or tooling to automate the preparation phase of FBM order fulfillment.
business_goals: Reduce time spent on morning order review and preparation by at least 50%
Eliminate duplicate processing of the same order
Reduce fulfillment mistakes caused by mixed SKUs and quantity miscalculations
Provide clear, grouped order views for warehouse packing staff
Surface orders needing manual review before fulfillment action
Lay a foundation for future real API integrations (Amazon SP-API, DHL, Easy Ship)

personas.name: Operations Assistant / Virtual Assistant
personas.description: A person responsible for reviewing unshipped FBM orders every morning and preparing them for fulfillment.
personas.need: Automatically synced, grouped, and prepared orders so the team can save time and reduce manual mistakes.

personas.name: Warehouse Packing Staff
personas.description: A person responsible for picking, packing, and preparing orders for shipment.
personas.need: Clear pick lists, pack slips, and shipping label batches so orders can be processed quickly and accurately.

personas.name: Operations Manager
personas.description: A person responsible for overseeing fulfillment accuracy, speed, exception handling, and operational risk.
personas.need: Visibility into sync status, duplicate prevention, errors, and orders needing review so fulfillment risk is controlled.

functional_requirements.title: Load mock unshipped FBM orders
functional_requirements.description: The system shall load a set of mock/sample Amazon.de FBM orders with realistic data (order IDs, SKUs, quantities, customer info, order dates) on application startup.

functional_requirements.title: Display orders in a grouped dashboard view
functional_requirements.description: The system shall display unshipped orders grouped by SKU and quantity patterns: (a) Single SKU with quantity 1, (b) Single SKU with quantity 2, (c) Single SKU with quantity 3+, (d) Mixed/multi-line orders.

functional_requirements.title: Flag orders requiring manual review
functional_requirements.description: The system shall automatically identify and flag orders that require manual review, including: mixed SKU orders, orders with unusually high quantities, and orders with identical SKUs listed on multiple lines.

functional_requirements.title: Prevent duplicate processing of orders
functional_requirements.description: The system shall track which orders have been processed (via local state/storage) and visually distinguish processed from unprocessed orders to prevent duplicate fulfillment.

functional_requirements.title: Allow manual marking of orders as reviewed
functional_requirements.description: The system shall allow operations staff to mark individual orders or groups of orders as 'Reviewed' after visual inspection.

functional_requirements.title: Display order item details
functional_requirements.description: The system shall show item-level details (SKU, product name, quantity, price) for each order, accessible via expanding or selecting the order.

functional_requirements.title: Show aggregate counts and summaries
functional_requirements.description: The system shall display summary statistics: total orders loaded, orders by group, orders needing review, and orders processed.

functional_requirements.title: Support filtering and sorting of orders
functional_requirements.description: The system shall allow users to filter orders by group type (single SKU, mixed, etc.) and by review status, and sort by order date or SKU.

functional_requirements.title: Log user actions and system events
functional_requirements.description: The system shall log key actions (order reviewed, order grouped, duplicate detected) and display them in a simple activity log panel.

functional_requirements.title: Provide a clear reset mechanism
functional_requirements.description: The system shall provide a reset action to clear processed state and reload fresh mock data for demonstration or restart purposes.

non_functional_requirements.title: Single-page application architecture
non_functional_requirements.description: The first implementation shall be a single-page application (SPA) that runs entirely in the browser with no server-side component.

non_functional_requirements.title: Client-side mock data only
non_functional_requirements.description: All order data shall be mock data embedded in the application. No real API calls to Amazon SP-API, DHL, or Easy Ship shall be made.

non_functional_requirements.title: Responsive dashboard layout
non_functional_requirements.description: The dashboard shall be usable on both desktop and tablet viewports to accommodate different staff workstations.

non_functional_requirements.title: Visual clarity and readability
non_functional_requirements.description: The UI shall use clear visual grouping, color coding, and typography to distinguish order categories and review status at a glance.

non_functional_requirements.title: Extensibility for future API integration
non_functional_requirements.description: The codebase shall be structured so that mock data providers can be easily replaced with real API service calls in the future.

non_functional_requirements.title: No authentication required
non_functional_requirements.description: The first implementation shall not include user authentication or role-based access control.

non_functional_requirements.title: Zero external runtime dependencies
non_functional_requirements.description: The application shall not depend on external network services at runtime. All data and logic shall be self-contained.

non_functional_requirements.title: Accessibility basics
non_functional_requirements.description: The dashboard shall follow basic accessibility practices (semantic HTML, ARIA labels, keyboard navigability) to support all staff.

in_scope: Mock Amazon.de FBM order data loaded locally
Dashboard displaying grouped orders (by SKU/quantity pattern)
Duplicate processing prevention via local state tracking
out_of_scope: Production PDF generation (pick lists, pack slips, labels)
User authentication and role-based access control
Automatic shipment confirmation back to Amazon

assumptions: Mock order data will be representative of real Amazon.de FBM order patterns (single SKU, multi-SKU, varying quantities)
The application will be built as a frontend-only SPA (React, Vue, or vanilla JS)
Users will access the dashboard via a modern web browser
Processed state can be tracked via in-memory state or localStorage (no backend database needed for first version)
The team has basic web development skills to extend the dashboard in future iterations
Future real API integrations will replace mock data providers through defined interfaces
The order grouping logic (by SKU/quantity pattern) is deterministic and can be implemented client-side

risks.description: Order grouping logic may not capture all real-world edge cases (e.g., same product listed under different SKUs)
risks.mitigation: Flag ambiguous groupings for manual review; make grouping rules configurable

risks.description: Mock data may not reflect the complexity of real Amazon.de orders
risks.mitigation: Design the mock data generator to produce varied, realistic scenarios; plan for real data validation in next iteration

risks.description: Duplicate prevention via local state may be lost on browser refresh or device switch
risks.mitigation: Use localStorage for persistence in first version; document that this is not production-grade

risks.description: Users may find the first version too limited without PDF or label generation
risks.mitigation: Clearly communicate that this is a foundation layer; demonstrate clear value in grouping and duplicate prevention alone

risks.description: The simple dashboard may not scale to hundreds of daily orders
risks.mitigation: Design for performance with basic pagination or virtual scrolling; document scaling path

epic.title: [Epic] Automate Morning FBM Order Fulfillment Preparation
epic.description: Automate the morning workflow for Amazon.de FBM order fulfillment preparation. Includes order syncing, SKU/quantity grouping, review identification, duplicate prevention, pick/pack document generation, and shipping label generation across multiple implementation cycles.
epic.label: epic

recommended_first_implementation.issue_title: [User Story] FBM Order Grouping & Review Dashboard (Mock Data)
recommended_first_implementation.description: As an operations assistant, I want a dashboard that loads mock unshipped Amazon.de FBM orders, groups them by SKU and quantity patterns, flags orders needing review, and prevents duplicate processing so that I can quickly assess the morning fulfillment workload.
recommended_first_implementation.label: user-story
acceptance_criteria_preview: Dashboard loads with 15-20 mock orders on startup
Orders are grouped into categories: Single Qty 1, Single Qty 2, Single Qty 3+, Mixed
Orders needing review are visually flagged with a distinct badge or color
Processed orders are tracked and visually distinguished from unprocessed
Clicking an order reveals item-level details
Summary counts are displayed at the top of the dashboard
Activity log records key user actions

payload_summary.business_intent: Automate Morning FBM Order Fulfillment Preparation
payload_summary.recommended_first_issue: FBM Order Grouping & Review Dashboard (Mock Data)
payload_summary.epic_candidate: [Epic] Automate Morning FBM Order Fulfillment Preparation
payload_summary.user_story_candidate: [User Story] FBM Order Grouping & Review Dashboard (Mock Data)
payload_summary.scope_evaluation: Full idea is Epic-level. First implementation is scoped to dashboard with mock data.
